INTERROGATORIES 216.1 AND 217.1 ARE DUPLICATES OF 16.1 AND 17.1 OF THE FORM INTERROGATORIES. THE PRO TEM FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF'S SERVICE OF THESE INTERROGATORIES WAS IN BAD FAITH. DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED AGAINST PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF $308.75 MADE PAYABLE TO DEFENDANT AND IS STAYED UNTIL THE NOVEMBER 12, 2012 HEARING BEFORE JUDGE PRO TEM KATHY GALLO. DISCOVERY 302, MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF CHARLES O'NEAL'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION TO ALEKSANDER A. VOKOV'S DECLARATION IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO PROVIDE A SEPARATE REPLY FOR THIS MOTION. INTERROGATORIES #1, 5, 6, 7: DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION TO THE TERM "WORKED FOR" IS SUSTAINED AND MODIFIED TO DESCRIBE ANY PERSON WHO WORKED FOR DEFENDANT IN AN EXEMPT OR NON-EXEMPT EMPLOYMENT CAPACITY. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION ON THE GROUNDS OF THIRD PARTY PRIVACY RIGHTS IS OVERRULED. THE NAMES AND LOCATIONS OF WITNESSES ARE DISCOVERABLE. SEE CCP SECTION 2017.010. DEFENDANT TO PROVIDE A FURTHER VERIFIED RESPONSE TO THIS REQUEST. INTERROGATORIES #2, 3, : DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION ON THE GROUNDS OF THIRD PARTY PRIVACY RIGHTS IS OVERRULED. THE NAMES AND LOCATIONS OF WITNESSES ARE DISCOVERABLE. SEE CCP SECTION 2017.010. DEFENDANT TO PROVIDE A FURTHER VERIFIED RESPONSE TO THIS REQUEST. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED AGAINST DEFENDANT AND HIS ATTORNEYS OF RECORD IN THE AMOUNT OF $1560.00 AND IS STAYED UNTIL THE NOVEMBER 12, 2012 HEARING BEFORE JUDGE PRO TEM KATHY GALLO. JUDGE PRO TEM: KATHERINE GALLO; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: MAXIMILLIAN A. CONTRERAS, CSR NO. 13876; DEPT. 302.

OBJECTIONS TO "DECLARATION OF RHETT T. FRANCISCO IN SUPPORT OF REPLY RE: ALL FOUR OF PLAINTIFF'S SEPTEMBER 30, 2014 MOTIONS TO COMPEL PERTAINING TO PLAINTIFF'S DISCOVERY, SET ONE" FILED BY DEFENDANT ARMADA PROTECTIVE SERVICES INC A CALIFORNIA CORP ORATION

DISCOVERY 302, MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF CHARLES O'NEAL'S REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT IS HELD AND CONTINUED FROM SEP-30-2014 TO NOV-12-2014, AT 9:00 AM, IN DEPT. 302, FOR FURTHER HEARING. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION TO ALEKSANDER A. VOLKOV'S DECLARATION IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO PROVIDE A SEPARATE REPLY FOR THIS MOTION. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION FILED ON SEPTEMBER 29, 2014 WASN'T CONSIDERED, AS IT WAS LATE. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION ON THE GROUNDS THAT THE TERM "EMPLOYMENT" CALLS FOR A LEGAL CONCLUSION IS OVERRULED. THE PRO-TEM IS NOT ABLE TO RULE ON THE CLAIMS OF FINANCIAL PRIVACY, PRIVACY OF THIRD-PARTIES, CLAIMS OF WORK PRODUCT AND ATTORNEY CLIENT PRIVILEGE WITHOUT A PRIVILEGE LOG. DEFENDANTS TO SERVE A FURTHER RESPONSE IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP SECTION 2031.220-240 WHICH INCLUDES A PRIVILEGE LOG IF ANY DOCUMENT IS BEING WITHHELD AND PRODUCE DOCUMENTS NO LATER THAN OCTOBER 21, 2014. IF ANY DEFENDANT NEED NOT LIST IN THE PRIVILEGE LOG ANY ATTORNEY-CLIENT COMMUNICATIONS OR ABSOLUTE WORK PRODUCT CREATED AFTER THE CLAIM WAS FILED WITH THE LABOR AND WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT AGENCY. IF PLAINTIFF FINDS THAT DEFENDANT'S FURTHER RESPONSES AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS ARE DEFICIENT, HE MAY FILE AND SERVE A SUPPLEMENTAL STATEMENT OF ITEMS IN DISPUTE IN A WORD DOCUMENT VIA E-MAIL NO LATER THAN OCTOBER 29, 2014. DEFENDANT TO ADD THEIR ARGUMENT AS TO WHY A FURTHER RESPONSE IS NOT REQUIRED TO THE ORIGINAL SEPARATE STATEMENT OF ITEMS IN DISPUTE. DOCUMENT TO BE FILED AND SERVED VIA E-MAIL ON PLAINTIFF COUNSEL AND JUDGE PRO TEM KATHY GALLO NO LATER THAN NOVEMBER 6, 2014. THE MATTER IS CONTINUED TO NOVEMBER 12, 2014 BEFORE JUDGE PRO TEM KATHY GALLO. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED AGAINST DEFENDANT IN THE AMOUNT OF $1560.00 AND IS STAYED UNTIL THE NOVEMBER 12, 2012 HEARING BEFORE JUDGE PRO TEM KATHY GALLO. JUDGE PRO TEM: KATHERINE GALLO; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER:

DECLARATION OF ALEKSANDR A. VOLKOV IN OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET NO. ONE, AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS FILED BY DEFENDANT ARMADA PROTECTIVE SERVICES INC A CALIFORNIA CORP ORATION

DISCOVERY 302, MTN TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES-GENERAL, SET ONE IS PARTIALLY GRANTED/PARTIALLY DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION TO ALEKSANDER A. VOKOV'S DECLARATION IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO PROVIDE A SEPARATE REPLY FOR THIS MOTION. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION IS GRANTED AND DENIED AS FOLLOW: INTERROGATORIES #3.1, 15.1, 17.1, 50.1-50.6: GRANTED. DEFENDANT TO PROVIDE A FURTHER RESPONSE TO EACH AND EVERY SUBPART. THE CODE REQUIRES THAT A PARTY MUST MAKE A REASONABLE AND GOOD FAITH EFFORT TO OBTAIN THE INFORMATION. REGENCY HEALTH SERVICES, INC. V. SUPERIOR COURT (1998) 64 CA4TH1496 "A PARTY CANNOT PLEAD IGNORANCE TO INFORMATION WHICH CAN BE OBTAINED FROM SOURCES UNDER HIS CONTROL." DEYO V. KILBOURNE (1978) 84 CA3D 771,782. THIS INCLUDES A PARTY'S LAWYER SMITH V. SUP. CT (ALFRED) (1961) 189 CA 2D 6, AGENTS OR EMPLOYEES GORDON V. SUP. CT. (U.Z.MFG.CO) (1984) 161 CA 3D 15,167-168, FAMILY MEMBERS JONES V. SUPERIOR COURT (BENNY) (1981) 119 CA 3D 534, 552 AND EXPERTS. SIGERSETH V. SUPERIOR COURT(1972) 23 CA 3D 427,433. SEE WEIL AND BROWN, CAL PRAC. GUIDE: CIVIL PROCEDURE BEFORE TRIAL (TRG 2014) 8:1051-1060. INTERROGATORIES #4.1, 12.1, 16.6 : DENIED. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS ON THE GROUNDS OF VAGUE AND AMBIGUOUS AS TO THE TERM "INCIDENT" IS SUSTAINED. THIS WAGE AND HOUR CLAIM CONSISTS OF MULTIPLE EVENTS AND A DEFINITION OF THE WORD "INCIDENT" IS NECESSARY. THIS RULING IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE. DEFENDANT TO SERVE VERIFIED RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES (GENERAL) INTERROGATORIES #3.1, 15.1, 17.1, 50.1-50.6 NO LATER THAN OCTOBER 21, 2014. ALL REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. JUDGE PRO TEM: KATHERINE GALLO; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: MAXIMILLIAN A. CONTRERAS, CSR NO. 13876; DEPT. 302.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff Charles O'Neal'S Special Interrogatories, Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant
Off Calendar Sep-30-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Rhett T. Francisco, of the Law Offices of Rhett T. Francisco, Tel. 818.319.9879, for plaintiffs and moving party, appearing via CourtCall;
Aleksandr A. Volkov, of Volkov Law Firm, Tel. 415.987.7000, for defendants and opposing party;
Court Reporter: Maximillian A. Contreras, CSR No. 13876, Tel. 415.421.3021;
No other appearances.

ARGUED. Plaintiff's objection to Aleksander A. Vokov's Declaration is overruled. Plaintiff failed to provide a separate reply for this motion.

Interrogatories #1, 5, 6, 7: Defendant's objection to the term "worked for" is sustained and modified to describe any person who worked for defendant in an exempt or non-exempt employment capacity. Defendant's objection on the grounds of third party privacy rights is overruled. The names and locations of witnesses are discoverable. See CCP Section 2017.010. Defendant to provide a further verified response to this request.

Interrogatories #2, 3, : Defendant's objections are overruled. Defendant's objection on the grounds of third party privacy rights is overruled. The names and locations of witnesses are discoverable. See CCP Section 2017.010. Defendant to provide a further verified response to this request.

Plaintiff's request for sanctions is granted against defendant and his attorneys of record in the amount of $1560.00 and is stayed until the November 12, 2012 hearing before Judge Pro Tem Kathy Gallo. Judge Pro Tem: Katherine Gallo; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Maximillian A. Contreras, CSR No. 13876; Dept. 302.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories-Employment Law, Set One And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-30-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Rhett T. Francisco, of the Law Offices of Rhett T. Francisco, Tel. 818.319.9879, for plaintiffs and moving party, appearing via CourtCall;
Aleksandr A. Volkov, of Volkov Law Firm, Tel. 415.987.7000, for defendants and opposing party;
Court Reporter: Maximillian A. Contreras, CSR No. 13876, Tel. 415.421.3021;
No other appearances.

ARGUED. Plaintiff's objection to Aleksander A. Volkov's Declaration is overruled. Plaintiff failed to provide a separate reply for this motion. Plaintiff's objection filed on September 29, 2014 wasn't considered, as it was late. PLAINTIFF CHARLES O'NEAL Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories-Employment Law, Set One is denied. Interrogatories 216.1 and 217.1 are duplicates of 16.1 and 17.1 of the form interrogatories. The pro tem finds that plaintiff's service of these interrogatories was in bad faith. Defendants' request for sanctions is granted against plaintiff in the amount of $308.75 made payable to defendant and is stayed until the November 12, 2012 hearing before Judge Pro Tem Kathy Gallo. Judge Pro Tem: Katherine Gallo; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Maximillian A. Contreras, CSR No. 13876; Dept. 302.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff Charles O'Neal'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant
Off Calendar Sep-30-2014 Continued To Nov-12-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Rhett T. Francisco, of the Law Offices of Rhett T. Francisco, Tel. 818.319.9879, for plaintiffs and moving party, appearing via CourtCall;
Aleksandr A. Volkov, of Volkov Law Firm, Tel. 415.987.7000, for defendants and opposing party;
Court Reporter: Maximillian A. Contreras, CSR No. 13876, Tel. 415.421.3021;
No other appearances.

ARGUED. Plaintiff's objection to Aleksander A. Volkov's Declaration is overruled. Plaintiff failed to provide a separate reply for this motion. Plaintiff's objection filed on September 29, 2014 wasn't considered, as it was late. Defendant's objection on the grounds that the term "employment" calls for a legal conclusion is overruled. The pro-tem is not able to rule on the claims of financial privacy, privacy of third-parties, claims of work product and attorney client privilege without a privilege log. Defendants to serve a further response in compliance with CCP Section 2031.220-240 which includes a privilege log if any document is being withheld and produce documents no later than October 21, 2014. If any Defendant need not list in the privilege log any attorney-client communications or absolute work product created after the claim was filed with the Labor and Workforce Development Agency.

If plaintiff finds that defendant's further responses and production of documents are deficient, he may file and serve a supplemental statement of items in dispute in a Word document via e-mail no later than October 29, 2014. Defendant to add their argument as to why a further response is not required to the original separate statement of items in dispute. Document to be filed and served via e-mail on plaintiff counsel and Judge Pro Tem Kathy Gallo no later than November 6, 2014. The matter is continued to November 12, 2014 before Judge Pro Tem Kathy Gallo.

Plaintiff's request for sanctions is granted against defendant in the amount of $1560.00 and is stayed until the November 12, 2012 hearing before Judge Pro Tem Kathy Gallo. Judge Pro Tem: Katherine Gallo; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Maximillian A. Contreras, CSR No. 13876; Dept. 302.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories-General, Set One And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-30-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Rhett T. Francisco, of the Law Offices of Rhett T. Francisco, Tel. 818.319.9879, for plaintiffs and moving party, appearing via CourtCall;
Aleksandr A. Volkov, of Volkov Law Firm, Tel. 415.987.7000, for defendants and opposing party;
Court Reporter: Maximillian A. Contreras, CSR No. 13876, Tel. 415.421.3021;
No other appearances.

ARGUED. Plaintiff's objection to Aleksander A. Vokov's Declaration is overruled. Plaintiff failed to provide a separate reply for this motion. Plaintiff's Motion is granted and denied as follow: Interrogatories #3.1, 15.1, 17.1, 50.1-50.6: granted. Defendant to provide a further response to each and every subpart. The code requires that a party must make a reasonable and good faith effort to obtain the information. Regency Health Services, Inc. v. Superior Court (1998) 64 CA4th1496 "A party cannot plead ignorance to information which can be obtained from sources under his control." Deyo v. Kilbourne (1978) 84 CA3d 771,782. This includes a party's lawyer Smith v. Sup. Ct (Alfred) (1961) 189 CA 2d 6, agents or employees Gordon v. Sup. Ct. (U.Z.MFG.Co) (1984) 161 CA 3d 15,167-168, family members Jones v. Superior Court (Benny) (1981) 119 CA 3d 534, 552 and experts. Sigerseth v. Superior Court(1972) 23 CA 3d 427,433. See Weil and Brown, Cal Prac. Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2014) 8:1051-1060

Interrogatories #4.1, 12.1, 16.6 : Denied. Defendant's objections on the grounds of vague and ambiguous as to the term "Incident" is sustained. This wage and hour claim consists of multiple events and a definition of the word "Incident" is necessary. This ruling is without prejudice.

Defendant to serve verified responses to Form Interrogatories (General) Interrogatories #3.1, 15.1, 17.1, 50.1-50.6 no later than October 21, 2014. All requests for sanctions are denied. Judge Pro Tem: Katherine Gallo; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Maximillian A. Contreras, CSR No. 13876; Dept. 302.
